description: Emission-size budgets (MT7). 0% slack: any probe that emits MORE than a recorded threshold fails test-lambda-baseline. Landing intended growth means reviewing the emission diff and lifting the number here in the SAME commit, so the growth is visible in review. A probe that emits LESS passes and prints the tightened values; committing them is the manual re-baseline. This file is never written by the test. Profiles are looked up as <platform>-<config>, then <platform>, then default -- a platform that legitimately emits differently (Windows commits side-stack pages in the prologue) gets its own explicit entry rather than silent slack. Instruction counts come from the finalized MIR artifact; frame shape comes from LAMBDA_MIR_LOG_FRAME_SLOTS telemetry, whose function names are a separate namespace (LambdaJS reports several emitted functions under one debug name), which is why 'functions' and 'frames' are addressed separately.

probes.name: lambda_scalar_home_donation
probes.description: Caller-donated scalar home: the donation protocol's instruction cost.

probes.name: lambda_scalar_home_tail_forward
probes.description: Tail-home forwarding, including the recursive body's root reservation.

probes.name: js_hoisted_modvar_write_through
probes.description: Nested closures over an IIFE scope: the shape most exposed to rooting bloat.

probes.name: js_corpus_side_stack_frame_gc
probes.description: The rooting-bloat sentinel: the GC-rooting regression script, which is the committed stand-in for the historical frameReview probe whose emission grew 58 -> 440 instructions under shadow-copy rooting.

probes.name: lambda_cow_nested_store
probes.description: Stage-1 nested COW relinking: 149 finalized instructions on the reviewed register/root-slot path, with no heap path-array construction.
